# Synthetic Markdown Document (1048576 byte target)

Generated feature-rich markdown for parser benchmarking. Contains headings, emphasis, lists, tables, code, blockquotes, task lists, strikethrough, images and thematic breaks.

---

## Section 1: the streaming buffer

This is paragraph **number 1** describing a *streaming* buffer. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/43) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **91% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *37.3ms* per document.

> Blockquote 1: "Performance is a feature." The buffer processed 43 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #43

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/1)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the buffer
  - Another nested point at 91% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 43
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/1.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (43 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<buffer>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 1)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 91% target on doc 43
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 1

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 805 | 5.2 | yes |
| league GFM | 22 | 47 | yes |
| tempest | 22 | 43 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `diff`:

```diff
# block 1: streaming buffer
run --threads 43 --target 91
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/1> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 43 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 30** describing a *zero-copy* parser. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/3) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **29% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *91.4ms* per document.

> Blockquote 30: "Performance is a feature." The parser processed 3 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #3

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/30)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the parser
  - Another nested point at 29% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 3
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/30.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (3 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<parser>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 30)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 29% target on doc 3
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 30

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 476 | 5.1 | yes |
| league GFM | 20 | 30 | yes |
| tempest | 57 | 75 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `sql`:

```sql
# block 30: zero-copy parser
run --threads 3 --target 29
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/30> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 3 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 59** describing a *zero-copy* renderer. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/97) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **26% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *24.7ms* per document.

> Blockquote 59: "Performance is a feature." The renderer processed 97 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #97

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/59)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the renderer
  - Another nested point at 26% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 97
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/59.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (97 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<renderer>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 59)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 26% target on doc 97
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 59

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 850 | 6 | yes |
| league GFM | 45 | 71 | yes |
| tempest | 31 | 79 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `c`:

```c
char *out = md2html(input, len, &out_len, flags, rflags); /* 59 */
if (!out) return -1; /* 26% of runs */
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/59> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 97 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 117** describing a *compiled* codepath. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/34) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **61% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *5.6ms* per document.

> Blockquote 117: "Performance is a feature." The codepath processed 34 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #34

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/117)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the codepath
  - Another nested point at 61% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 34
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/117.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (34 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<codepath>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 117)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 61% target on doc 34
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 117

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 832 | 3.6 | yes |
| league GFM | 66 | 81 | yes |
| tempest | 66 | 60 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `sql`:

```sql
# block 117: compiled codepath
run --threads 34 --target 61
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/117> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 34 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 219** describing a *blazing* parser. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/39) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **21% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *7.9ms* per document.

> Blockquote 219: "Performance is a feature." The parser processed 39 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #39

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/219)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the parser
  - Another nested point at 21% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 39
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/219.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (39 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<parser>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 219)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 21% target on doc 39
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 219

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 591 | 2.4 | yes |
| league GFM | 29 | 57 | yes |
| tempest | 40 | 68 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `bash`:

```bash
# block 219: blazing parser
run --threads 39 --target 21
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/219> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 39 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 248** describing a *streaming* parser. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/5) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **39% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *68.2ms* per document.

> Blockquote 248: "Performance is a feature." The parser processed 5 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #5

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/248)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the parser
  - Another nested point at 39% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 5
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/248.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (5 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<parser>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 248)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 39% target on doc 5
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 248

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 709 | 3.4 | yes |
| league GFM | 65 | 67 | yes |
| tempest | 23 | 79 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `go`:

```go
# block 248: streaming parser
run --threads 5 --target 39
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/248> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 5 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 277** describing a *streaming* parser. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/47) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **83% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *60ms* per document.

> Blockquote 277: "Performance is a feature." The parser processed 47 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #47

    - Deeply nested leaf node 47
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/277.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (47 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<parser>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 277)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 83% target on doc 47
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 277

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 496 | 2.3 | yes |
| league GFM | 61 | 65 | yes |
| tempest | 26 | 46 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `bash`:


This is paragraph **number 465** describing a *vectorized* shim. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/7) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **93% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *36.2ms* per document.

> Blockquote 465: "Performance is a feature." The shim processed 7 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #7

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/465)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the shim
  - Another nested point at 93% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 7
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/465.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (7 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<shim>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 465)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 93% target on doc 7
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 465

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 820 | 5 | yes |
| league GFM | 32 | 44 | yes |
| tempest | 25 | 41 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `c`:

```c
char *out = md2html(input, len, &out_len, flags, rflags); /* 465 */
if (!out) return -1; /* 93% of runs */
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/465> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 7 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

This is paragraph **number 494** describing a *allocator-aware* parser. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/48) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **50% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *22.1ms* per document.

> Blockquote 494: "Performance is a feature." The parser processed 48 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #48

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/494)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the parser
  - Another nested point at 50% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 48
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/494.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (48 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<parser>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 494)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 50% target on doc 48
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 494

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 762 | 3.8 | yes |
| league GFM | 24 | 76 | yes |
| tempest | 68 | 64 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `c`:

```c

if (!out) return -1; /* 50% of runs */
```

This is paragraph **number 523** describing a *vectorized* buffer. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/61) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **15% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *68.6ms* per document.

> Blockquote 523: "Performance is a feature." The buffer processed 61 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #61

- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***

    - Deeply nested leaf node 61
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/523.png)

1. Allocate the arena (61 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<buffer>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 523)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 15% target on doc 61
- [ ] Publish the results

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 404 | 4.1 | yes |
| league GFM | 44 | 52 | yes |
| tempest | 52 | 59 | partial |

This is paragraph **number 552** describing a *blazing* arena. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/16) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **83% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *99.2ms* per document.

> Blockquote 552: "Performance is a feature." The arena processed 16 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #16

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/552)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the arena
  - Another nested point at 83% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 16
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/552.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (16 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<arena>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 552)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 83% target on doc 16
- [ ] Publish the results

### Benchmark table 552

| Engine | Throughput (MB/s) | Memory (MB) | Correct |
|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
| md4c (FFI) | 452 | 5.8 | yes |
| league GFM | 53 | 37 | yes |
| tempest | 68 | 103 | partial |

Here is a fenced code block in `php`:

```php
$html = (new MarkdownFight\Parser())->render($input); // doc 552
assert(strlen($html) > 16);
```

A final paragraph with an autolink <https://autolink.test/552> and a footnote-ish aside. Inline `code spans` survive 16 round-trips. Some &amp; entity &copy; handling and a backslash escape \* here.

## Section 596: the compiled dispatcher

This is paragraph **number 596** describing a *compiled* dispatcher. It runs in `O(n)` time and handles [external links](https://example.com/page/21) alongside ~~deprecated~~ APIs. We measured a **93% speedup** over the baseline implementation, with a tail latency of *29ms* per document.

> Blockquote 596: "Performance is a feature." The dispatcher processed 21 thousand nodes without a single heap allocation in the hot loop.
>
> — anonymous benchmark, run #21

### Unordered features

- First item with `inline code` and a [link](http://x.test/596)
- Second item is **bold** and *italic* combined into ***both***
- Third item with nested detail:
  - Nested point about the dispatcher
  - Another nested point at 93% coverage
    - Deeply nested leaf node 21
- Fourth item with ~~struck text~~ and an ![inline image](https://img.test/596.png)

### Ordered steps

1. Allocate the arena (21 KB)
2. Tokenize the input stream
3. Render to HTML
   1. Escape entities
   2. Emit `<dispatcher>` tags
4. Free the arena in one shot

### Task list (run 596)

- [x] Compile the shared library
- [x] Wire up FFI bindings
- [ ] Beat the 93% target on doc 21
- [ ] Publish the results

|:-------|------------------:|:-----------:|:-------:|
